Pattern: Use modular type scale with fluid sizing
Description: Use clamp() for headings, fewer sizes with more contrast (5-size system)
Never use Inter, Roboto, Arial, Open Sans, system defaults for distinctive design
Don't use monospace typography as lazy shorthand for technical/developer vibes

Pattern: No large rounded-corner icons above headings
Description: Large icons with rounded corners above headings rarely add value and look templated
Use rem/em not px for body text to respect user browser settings
Smaller than 16px strains eyes and fails WCAG on mobile
font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums for data tables and number alignment
Use text-wrap: balance or text-pretty on headings to prevent widows
Use oklch, color-mix, light-dark for perceptually uniform palettes
Even subtle tinting creates subconscious cohesion

Pattern: No gray text on colored backgrounds
Description: Gray looks washed out on color; use a shade of the background color instead
Never use #000 or #fff; always tint slightly as pure values don't appear in nature
Avoid cyan-on-dark, purple-to-blue gradients, neon accents on dark backgrounds
Gradient text on metrics/headings is decorative rather than meaningful

Pattern: No dark mode as default shortcut
Description: Don't default to dark mode with glowing accents to avoid actual design decisions
60% neutrals, 30% secondary, 10% accent. Accent works because it's rare
4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components

Pattern: Dark mode is not inverted light mode
Description: Different decisions needed: lighter surfaces for depth, desaturate accents, reduce font weight

Pattern: Create visual rhythm through varied spacing
Description: Tight groupings, generous separations - not same padding everywhere
Break the grid intentionally for emphasis
Not everything needs a container; spacing creates grouping naturally
Cards inside cards create visual noise; flatten the hierarchy
Same-sized cards with icon + heading + text repeated endlessly is monotonous
Big number, small label, supporting stats, gradient accent is generic AI pattern
Left-aligned text with asymmetric layouts feels more designed
4pt system for granularity: 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64, 96px
Use @container for component-level responsiveness instead of viewport queries
Interactive elements must meet minimum touch target size
Blur effects and glass cards used decoratively rather than purposefully is AI slop

Pattern: No rounded elements with thick colored border
Description: Colored border on one side is a lazy accent that rarely looks intentional
Tiny charts that look sophisticated but convey nothing meaningful

Pattern: No rounded rectangles with generic drop shadows
Description: Safe, forgettable, could be any AI output
Modals are lazy; consider alternatives first
Decorative elements should reinforce brand not just add visual noise
ease-out-quart/quint/expo for natural deceleration, not CSS defaults
They feel dated and tacky; real objects decelerate smoothly
Everything else causes layout recalculation
Never animate width, height, padding, margin directly
Use grid-template-rows: 0fr to 1fr instead of animating height
Always provide non-animated alternatives
Button press, toggle, color change
Menu open, tooltip, hover states
Use ~75% of enter duration for exits
Start simple, reveal sophistication through interaction
Empty states should teach the interface not just say nothing here
Don't repeat same info; redundant headers, intros restating heading
Use ghost buttons, text links, secondary styles; hierarchy matters
Removing focus indicators is an accessibility violation
Show focus ring only for keyboard users not mouse/touch
Always use visible label elements; placeholders disappear on input
Users click through confirmations mindlessly; undo is better UX
Start with base styles for mobile, use min-width queries to layer complexity
Let content tell you where to break not device sizes

Pattern: Detect input method not just screen size
Description: Use @media (pointer: fine/coarse) and (hover: hover/none) queries
Use env(safe-area-inset-*) for modern phone notches and home indicators

Pattern: Don't hide core functionality on mobile
Description: Adapt the interface don't amputate it
Save changes not OK, Create account not Submit
What happened + why + how to fix it in every error message
Acknowledge briefly, explain value, provide clear action
Reframe: Please enter MM/DD/YYYY format not You entered an invalid date
Pick one term and stick with it: Delete not Delete/Remove/Trash
Screen readers need text alternatives for icon buttons

Pattern: Form controls need label or aria-label
Description: Every form input must have an associated label
Use button, a, label, table before adding ARIA roles
user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1 breaks accessibility
onPaste with preventDefault is a hostile anti-pattern
Explicit dimensions prevent CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)

Pattern: Destructive actions need confirmation or undo
Description: Never immediately execute destructive actions
Lists >50 items should use virtualization (virtua, content-visibility: auto)
Avoid getBoundingClientRect, offsetHeight in render cycle
Add link rel=preconnect for CDN and asset domains
link rel=preload as=font with font-display: swap
List CSS transition properties explicitly
